steps.title: Recall the no-solution condition
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has no solution when the lines are parallel: same slope, different $y$-intercepts. So the second line must have the same slope as the equation given in the figure.

steps.title: Find the slope of the given equation
steps.content: The equation given in the problem stem can be written in slope-intercept form $y = mx + b$. Isolating $y$: starting from $27ry - 7x = c$ (where $c$ is a constant), add $7x$ to both sides and divide by $27r$ to get $y = \dfrac{7}{27r}x + \dfrac{c}{27r}$. The slope is $\dfrac{7}{27r}$. For the system to have no solution, the other equation must also have slope $\dfrac{7}{27r}$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "$-\frac{27r}{7}$": This is the negative reciprocal of $\dfrac{7}{27r}$, which would make the lines perpendicular (one solution), not parallel. ✗
  • "$-\frac{27}{7r}$": Negative and structurally different from $\dfrac{7}{27r}$; lines would intersect at one point. ✗
  • "$\frac{7r}{27}$": Close but the $r$ is in the numerator instead of the denominator, making this a different value. Lines would intersect. ✗
  • "$\frac{7}{27r}$": Matches the slope of the given equation exactly. Parallel lines, no solution. ✓
That matches the correct answer.
